name: Tangs — Acanthurus
description: Surgeonfish notorious for same-genus aggression. Powder Blue is the most assertive.

name: Tangs — Zebrasoma
description: Disc-shaped tangs. Same-genus pairings (Yellow + Purple) fight without serious tank size.

name: Tangs — Ctenochaetus
description: Bristletooth tangs. Peaceful with most fish but territorial with conspecifics.

name: Tangs — Naso
description: Largest tangs, most peaceful genus. Need significant tank volume.

name: Tangs — Paracanthurus
description: Single species genus. Active swimmer, single per tank typically.

name: Dwarf Angels — Centropyge
description: Small angels. Pairs/trios possible in 100g+ when added simultaneously. Flame is most aggressive.

name: Large Angels — Pomacanthus
description: Showpiece angels. Single per tank typically; juveniles transition aggressive at maturity.

name: Large Angels — Holacanthus
description: Caribbean and Pacific large angels. Rock Beauty has dietary specialization.

name: Genicanthus Angels
description: Planktivorous swallowtail angels. Most peaceful angel genus. Harems work in large tanks.

name: Wrasses — Halichoeres
description: Active reef-safe wrasses. Different species can mix; same-species typically pair-only.

name: Wrasses — Cirrhilabrus (Fairy)
description: Fairy wrasses. Compatibility is complex-dependent (see Cirrhilabrus rule book).

name: Wrasses — Paracheilinus (Flasher)
description: Flasher wrasses. Multi-male harems work in 75g+ when added together.

name: Wrasses — Macropharyngodon (Leopard)
description: Leopard wrasses. Sand bed required. Sensitive to bullying — added simultaneously, large tank.

name: Wrasses — Pseudocheilinus
description: Six Line and relatives. Aggressive to conspecifics and bullies more delicate wrasses.

name: Wrasses — Anampses
description: Sensitive wrasses, sand bed required. Added together when keeping multiples.

name: Hogfish — Bodianus
description: Larger wrasses. Older specimens predate inverts and small fish.

name: Damsels — Chrysiptera
description: Damsel genus. Springeri/Talbot/Rolland's most peaceful. Yellowtail (parasema) more aggressive.

name: Chromis
description: Schooling damsels. Keep odd-numbered groups (5+); pair-off culling is normal.

name: Clownfish
description: Pair only, conspecific only. Maroon especially aggressive. Cross-species rarely tolerates.

name: Gobies — Cryptocentrus / Stonogobiops
description: Shrimp-pair gobies. Territorial; pair only when shrimp-paired together.

name: Gobies — Gobiodon / Eviota
description: Tiny perching gobies. Peaceful, multiples OK.

name: Gobies — Nemateleotris (Firefish/Dartfish)
description: Peaceful midwater dartfish. Pair or group when added together. Easy prey for hawks/triggers.

name: Gobies — Valenciennea (Sand-sifters)
description: Sand-sifting gobies. Territorial with conspecifics. Compete with mandarins for pods.

name: Blennies — Salarias / Ecsenius
description: Algae-grazing blennies. Territorial with conspecifics; peaceful otherwise.

name: Blennies — Meiacanthus (Fang)
description: Fang blennies. Mildly venomous fangs deter predators. Pair-able.

name: Cardinalfish
description: Mid-water schooling fish. Banggai pair/group; introduce together.

name: Anthias — Pseudanthias
description: Single-species harems (1 male + 3+ females). Heavy feeders, demanding.

name: Triggerfish — Peaceful
description: Reef-safer triggers. Single per tank. Some reef-compatible with caution.

name: Triggerfish — Aggressive
description: Aggressive triggers. Single only. FOWLR-only.

name: Lionfish — Pterois
description: Predators. Eat any fish that fits in their mouth. Venomous spines.

name: Eels — Muraenidae
description: Predators. Eat fish/inverts. Single typically.

name: Groupers — Cephalopholis (small)
description: Smaller groupers for home aquaria. Predators. Single only.

name: Hawkfish — Cirrhitidae
description: Perching predators. Eat shrimp and small fish. Territorial.

name: Puffers — Tetraodontidae
description: Will nip coral and inverts. Single typically. Larger species need huge tanks.

name: Royal Gramma / Grammas
description: Cave-dwelling fish. Territorial with conspecifics and similar-niche fish.

name: Dottybacks / Pseudochromis
description: Aggressive for size. Peaceful exceptions: Elongatus, Coccinacauda.

name: Basslets — Liopropoma
description: Cave-dwelling basslets. Peaceful but reclusive. Single typically.

name: Butterflyfish — Reef Safe
description: Coral-tolerant butterflies. Copperband eats Aiptasia. Pyramids work in groups.

name: Butterflyfish — Non Reef Safe
description: Most Chaetodon will nip corals/clams. FOWLR-only generally.

name: Mandarins / Dragonets — Synchiropus
description: Pod-eaters. Mature reef with copious copepods required. Single or M+F pair.

name: Rabbitfish / Foxface — Siganus
description: Algae-grazing reef fish with venomous dorsal spines for defense. Peaceful with most fish but can be territorial with other rabbitfish and surgeonfish-niche species. Good tang alternative or co-pilot in larger systems.
